name: Doc West
abstract: Harold "Doc" West (August 12, 1915 – May 4, 1951) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Harold West and I came to -- had an arrangement where he played some of my sets on one Sunday while I cooked, and I'd reciprocate and cover him on his sets the following Sunday while he cooked.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: I was in the house band up there. You know, there was me and Thelonious Monk and Nick Fenton and Harold West and Klock sometimes, Kenny Clark. I was in the house group.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: And we became very close friends ever after that. He and I and Doc West used to live in the same place... Doc was the official cook for the group... we had a ball.

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: Tiny had a good little band. ... and the drummer Harold was West -- Eddie West -- not Harold West -- Eddie West, I think, was the drummer from Detroit.

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Eldridge
about_quotes.evidence: And my band, like... It was John Collins, Harold West, Gabby(?), Chuck and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: It was Prez, Clyde Hart, Doc West, and Shad Collins. It was a good band.
